concepts.concept_name: Meaning of Consumer and Consumer Exploitation
concepts.lesson: A consumer is a person who buys goods or services for personal use. Consumer exploitation occurs when sellers engage in unfair practices such as overcharging, selling defective goods, or providing misleading information.
concepts.real_example: A shopkeeper charging more than the printed MRP is an example of exploitation.
key_points: Consumer buys goods or services for personal use

concepts.lesson: Consumers are protected by specific rights such as the Right to Safety, Right to Information, Right to Choose, Right to Seek Redressal, and Right to Consumer Education. These rights ensure fair treatment in the marketplace.
concepts.real_example: Checking expiry date before buying medicine is an example of exercising the Right to Information.
key_points: Right to Safety protects from hazardous goods
Right to Information ensures full product details
Right to Seek Redressal provides compensation

concepts.lesson: Unfair trade practices include misleading advertisements, underweight goods, adulteration, and false claims about product quality. These practices harm consumers and violate their rights.
concepts.real_example: An advertisement claiming unrealistic health benefits is misleading.

concepts.concept_name: Consumer Protection Act and Consumer Courts
concepts.lesson: The Consumer Protection Act was enacted to safeguard consumer interests. Consumer disputes are handled by District Forums, State Commissions, and the National Commission based on the value of claims.
concepts.real_example: A consumer can file a complaint in the District Forum for defective products.
key_points: Consumer Protection Act ensures legal support

concepts.lesson: Quality certification marks such as ISI, Agmark, Hallmark, and FSSAI ensure product standards and safety. These marks help consumers make informed decisions.
concepts.real_example: Hallmark on gold jewellery indicates purity certification.

concepts.lesson: Consumer awareness is essential to prevent exploitation. Consumers must check product details, demand bills, and report unfair practices. Along with rights, consumers also have responsibilities.
concepts.real_example: Keeping the bill safely helps while filing a complaint.
key_points: Demand bill and check expiry date
